findings.detail: Capex as % of Revenue surged from 10.49% (FY2023) to 15.01% (FY2024) to 22.70% (FY2025). In absolute terms, capex nearly tripled from $32.3B to $91.4B over two years, while revenue grew only 31%. This is a dramatic acceleration of capital deployment, consistent with known AI infrastructure investments. Not a data error, but a material business inflection.

ROIC peaked in FY2024 at 27.94%, then declined to 25.39% in FY2025, despite net margin expanding from 28.6% to 32.81%. The gap reflects massive capex deployment (91.4B in FY2025) whose returns have not yet manifested. This is a characteristic pattern of a heavy investment phase and not inherently concerning, but signals near-term ROIC pressure until capex cycle normalizes or new assets generate returns.

findings.metric: Free Cash Flow Margin & Net Debt Position
findings.detail: FCF margin compressed from 22.61% (FY2023) to 18.19% (FY2025) despite operating margin holding steady near 32%. Simultaneously, net debt position flipped from net cash of $10.1B to net debt of $17.8B. Both shifts are driven by the capex ramp. Alphabet is simultaneously reducing share count ($61.5B buybacks in FY2023 → $45.7B in FY2025), issuing dividends ($0 → $10.0B), and leveraging balance sheet for capex—a deliberate capital allocation shift.

Operating margin ticked down from 32.11% (FY2024) to 32.03% (FY2025) despite revenue growth of 15.09% and gross margin expansion to 59.65%. This small decline is likely attributable to increased depreciation & amortization on the new capex base (91.4B deployed in FY2025 begins hitting the P&L). Not a red flag, but marks a shift from the 2023–2024 margin expansion period.

Interest coverage ratio fell sharply from 419.4x (FY2024) to 175.3x (FY2025) due to total debt increasing from $14.2B to $48.5B. Coverage remains extremely healthy and is not a solvency concern, but the trend reflects the debt issuance supporting capex.

Q1 2023 shares outstanding is NaN. This does not affect annual figures (present and accurate) and is isolated to one early quarter, likely due to reporting lag. Low impact: Q2–Q4 2023 and all subsequent quarters are complete.
